extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a photo-finish sprint after Jumbo-Visma's VAN AERT pulled 2km at 65kph to set up the leadout.
tactical_patterns: JUMBO-VISMA used cyclocross world champion VAN AERT for 2km at 65kph despite WYNANT still recovering back after intermediate sprint gap.
BAHRAIN-MERIDA deliberately accelerated intermediate sprint at 33km-to-go, catching MARTIN Dan and QUINTANA Nairo off guard for second time in week.
DECEUNINCK-QUICKSTEP's MORKOV led final kilometer at extremely high pace but launched VIVIANI too early at 200m, fading badly in final meters.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Demonstrated extraordinary diesel power pulling leadout for 2km at 65kph in debut Tour, confirming road transfer from cyclocross elite.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: Caught out at intermediate sprint acceleration for second time in seven stages, suggesting poor positioning instincts or team support.
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Dropped prematurely on Stage 6 mountain finish, then crashed badly Stage 7, indicating deteriorating form trajectory.
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Faded dramatically after being launched 200m out by strong Quickstep train, suggesting either poor timing or lacking finishing punch.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Looking ominously strong on Stage 6 mountains despite only 6 seconds from yellow, suggesting GC threat rather than puncheur role.
